notableMoments: Qualified for the 2026 World Cup through the intercontinental playoff, marking their return to the world stage after 52 years — the longest gap between World Cup appearances in history
First appeared at the World Cup in 1974 as Zaire, becoming only the third sub-Saharan African nation to qualify for the tournament
The 1974 World Cup campaign is remembered for a 9-0 defeat to Yugoslavia and an infamous moment when defender Ilunga Mwepu kicked the ball away before a Brazilian free-kick
Won the Africa Cup of Nations twice, in 1968 and 1974 (as Zaire/Congo), establishing themselves as one of the continent's historic football powers
DR Congo's passionate fanbase, known for their incredible support and drumming, are one of the most vibrant in African football
The current squad features a blend of European-based professionals led by Chancel Mbemba, Yoane Wissa, and Cédric Bakambu with homegrown talent from the domestic league
Reached the semifinal of the 2023 Africa Cup of Nations, their best continental result in decades, signaling their return as a force in African football
The 2026 World Cup represents a chance for redemption after the painful memories of 1974, with a far more competitive squad assembled from top European leagues
